one_liner: SOUN flat at $6.40 — RSI 26.8 is deep oversold, below lower BB, Wyckoff Accumulation Phase B; no DCF feasibility and this is purely a voice AI speculative name.

price_action.intraday_story: SOUN dipped to $6.15 (testing the lower BB at $5.57) then recovered to close at $6.40. RSI at 26.8 is deep oversold — any further selling into $5.57–$6.00 could trigger a bounce.

news_catalyst.headline: No Significant SOUN News — Voice AI Sector Quiet
news_catalyst.summary: No specific SOUN news. SoundHound continues to build its voice AI platform for automotive and enterprise clients. The oversold RSI at 26.8 suggests significant recent selling — likely macro risk-off in AI speculative names.

technical.summary: RSI 26.8 is the second-most oversold reading in the watchlist (after GOOG's 27.95 on June 26). Below EMA50 ($8.03) and EMA200 ($8.87) — full bearish EMA alignment. The Wyckoff Phase B bullish tag combined with RSI <30 suggests this could be near a local bottom, but the downtrend hasn't been broken.

wyckoff_narrative: Phase B accumulation at extreme oversold RSI. The $5.57 lower BB is the key support level. A spike to that level on elevated volume followed by recovery would be the Wyckoff Spring — the final shakeout that ends Phase B and begins Phase C/D.

valuation.summary: DCF not feasible — pre-revenue AI voice platform. Value is entirely optionality.

key_strengths: Voice AI technology with automotive OEM partnerships
Strategic partnerships with automotive majors provide recurring contract revenue
key_concerns: Significant dilution risk — frequent equity raises
fundamentals.summary: Grade C. Speculative voice AI at extreme oversold levels. Not investment-grade but RSI 26.8 warrants monitoring for a bounce trade setup.

risks: RSI 26.8 oversold but downtrend still intact — catching a falling knife risk
EMA50 at $8.03 is 25% overhead — full recovery is a long road
